items.title: Football: France beat Colombia 3 days after defeating Brazil before World Cup
items.summary: France comfortably beat Colombia in a friendly match in the United States (3-1). This win caps off a successful tour in the United States three days after victory over Brazil.

items.title: Russian oil tanker nears Cuba, Trump softens de facto blockade rhetoric
items.summary: A ​Russian oil tanker entered Cuban waters on Monday, possibly granting a lifeline to Cuba as US President Donald Trump appeared to relax a de facto oil blockade on ‌the Caribbean island. FRANCE 24's Ed Augustin reports from Havana.

items.title: Israel restores Latin Patriarch’s access to Holy Sepulchre church after international outcry
items.summary: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Monday said the Latin Patriarch of Jerusalem now has "full and immediate access" to the Church of the Holy Sepulchre after Cardinal Pierbattista Pizzaballa was blocked from holding Palm Sunday Mass at Christianity's holiest site, triggering an international outcry.

items.title: Murder trial involving Freemasons, French secret agents opens in Paris court
items.summary: A Paris court on Monday began hearings in a major trial involving 22 suspects accused of murder and other serious crimes on behalf of a mafia network inside the Athanor Masonic Lodge in the Paris suburb of Puteaux. The accused include police officers, former French intelligence agents and businessmen.

items.title: Amnesty warns 2026 World Cup across North America risks becoming a ‘stage for repression’
items.summary: Amnesty International warned on Monday that this summer’s FIFA World Cup in the United States, Canada and Mexico risks becoming a “stage for repression”, urging FIFA and host nations to protect the human rights of players and fans.

items.title: Middle East war live: Trump says he could seize Iran's Kharg Island as US ramps up troop buildup
items.summary: US ⁠President ​Donald ​Trump said he wants ​to "take ‌the ⁠oil ‌in Iran" ⁠and could seize ​the ‌export hub of  Kharg ​Island, in an interview with ​Financial ​Times ​published on ​Sunday as about 2,500 US Marines arrived in the region. Follow our liveblog for the latest developments.

items.title: Russian tanker nears blockaded Cuba, Trump shrugs off ‘a boat of oil’
items.summary: A ​Russian oil tanker entered Cuban waters on Monday despite a de facto US fuel blockade as President Donald Trump has turned up the political pressure on ‌the Caribbean island in recent days. But on Sunday, Trump appeared to relax his tone, claiming the Russian tanker was just "a boat of oil" and its cargo of 730,000 barrels of crude was "not going to matter".

items.title: European nations say Israel’s planned expansion of death penalty is a "grave step backwards"
items.summary: Britain, France, Germany and Italy on Sunday voiced deep concern over Israeli plans to expand the use of the death penalty in a bill expected to be voted on next week. The Council of Europe’s rights body also opposed the draft law, warning it would significantly broaden capital punishment.

items.title: African football chief Motsepe to respect CAS ruling on Senegal’s appeal
items.summary: Confederation of African Football (CAF) president Patrice Motsepe on Sunday said he would implement the Court of Arbitration for Sport (CAS) decision on Senegal national football team’s appeal after they were stripped of their Africa Cup of Nations title, as the governing body moves to restore confidence following the controversial final.

items.title: Pakistan offers to host US-Iran talks as Tehran warns ground troops would be 'set on fire'
items.summary: Pakistan on Sunday said it would soon host talks between the United States and Iran over the monthlong war. Pakistan aims to position itself as a mediator as fighting intensifies across the region, global energy routes are shaken and fears grow of a broader conflict involving Israel, Lebanon and key maritime chokepoints.

items.title: Artémis II : décollage d'une nouvelle course à la Lune
items.summary: Après plusieurs reports et retards, la mission Artémis II devrait décoller dans la nuit du mercredi 1er au jeudi 2 avril. Pour la première fois depuis 50 ans, elle entraînera des humains autour de la Lune, avant une prochaine étape : fouler à nouveau le sol lunaire. Analyse des enjeux de cette mission pas comme les autres.

translation.title: Artemis II: taking off a new moon race
translation.summary: After several postponements and delays, the Artemis II mission is expected to take off in the night from Wednesday 1st to Thursday 2nd April. For the first time in 50 years, it will cause humans around the Moon, before a next step: trample the lunar soil again. Analysis of the stakes of this mission not like the others.

items.title: Procès Athanor : des francs-maçons aux pratiques criminelles devant les assises de Paris
items.summary: Vingt-deux accusés, âgés de 30 à 73 ans, sont jugés à partir de lundi et pour plus de trois mois devant la cour d'assises de Paris pour meurtre, tentatives de meurtre, violences aggravées et association de malfaiteurs, dans le cadre du procès Athanor – du nom d'une loge maçonnique des Hauts-de-Seine.

translation.title: Athanor Trial: From Freemasons to Criminal Practices in front of the Paris Foundations
translation.summary: Twenty-two accused, between the ages of 30 and 73, are tried from Monday and for more than three months in the Paris Assize Court for murder, attempted murder, aggravated violence and criminal association, in the context of the Athanor trial – named after a Masonic lodge in Hauts-de-Seine.

items.title: La France bat la Colombie grâce à ses remplaçants pour conclure sa tournée américaine
items.summary: L’équipe de France, largement remaniée, a facilement dominé la Colombie dimanche en match amical aux États-Unis (3-1), grâce notamment à un doublé de Désiré Doué. C'est le deuxième succès des Bleus en trois jours après celui contre le Brésil (2-1), de bon augure avant le Mondial 2026

translation.title: France beats Colombia with its replacements to conclude its American tour

items.title: Iran : l'armée israélienne dit mener des frappes sur Téhéran
items.summary: L'armée israélienne a dit mener des frappes sur des infrastructures militaires à Téhéran, alors que la guerre au Moyen-Orient est entrée dans son deuxième mois. Le ministère iranien de l'Énergie a signalé des coupures de courant dans la capitale et sa région. L'Iran poursuit de son côté ses frappes à titre de représailles contre des intérêts américains et économiques dans le Golfe.

translation.title: Iran: Israeli Army Says Strikes on Tehran · Global Voices
translation.summary: The Israeli army said that it was carrying out strikes on military infrastructure in Tehran, while the war in the Middle East entered its second month. The Iranian Ministry of Energy reported power cuts in the capital and its region. Iran, for its part, continues its strikes as reprisals against American and economic interests in the Gulf.

items.title: Messe interdite à Jérusalem: "Bibi vous souhaite de joyeuses Pâques"
items.summary: A la Une de la presse, ce lundi 30 mars, les réactions à la décision de la police israélienne d’empêcher le patriarche latin de Jérusalem d’accéder au Saint-Sépulcre, hier, pour la messe des Rameaux. Le gouvernement israélien critiqué par l’Italie, mais aussi la France, l’Allemagne, et le Royaume-Uni, pour un projet de loi visant à élargir le recours à la peine de mort. Les témoignages d'Iraniens sous les bombes. Et ces expressions que l'on doit à Jean de La Fontaine.

translation.title: Holy Mass forbidden in Jerusalem: "Bibi wishes you happy Easter"
translation.summary: On the front page of the press, this Monday 30 March, reactions to the decision of the Israeli police to prevent the Latin Patriarch of Jerusalem from accessing the Holy Sepulchre yesterday for the Palm Mass. The Israeli government criticized Italy, but also France, Germany and the United Kingdom for a bill to extend the use of the death penalty. The testimony of Iranians under the bombs. And those expressions that we owe to Jean de La Fontaine.

items.title: Serbie : le président proclame la victoire de son parti lors de municipales sous tension
items.summary: Des élections municipales étaient convoquées dans dix localités en Serbie et ont été émaillées de nombreux incidents. Bien que partiel, ce scrutin revêt une importance pour le mouvement étudiant, qui a proposé ses propres listes, comme pour le président Aleksandar Vucic. Dès dimanche soir, il a déclaré la victoire de son parti, le SNS.

translation.title: Serbia: President proclaims victory of his party in busy municipalities
translation.summary: Municipal elections were held in ten localities in Serbia and were overshadowed by numerous incidents. Although partial, this election is important for the student movement, which has proposed its own lists, as for President Aleksandar Vucic. On Sunday night, he declared the victory of his party, the SNS.

items.title: Plusieurs pays européens s'inquiètent du projet de loi israélien sur la peine de mort
items.summary: Le projet de loi israélien visant à étendre la peine de mort doit être soumis au vote à la Knesset lundi. Un texte qui préoccupe les ministres des Affaires étrangères de l'Allemagne, de la France, de l'Italie et du Royaume-Uni, qui estiment que ce texte "risquerait de remettre en cause les engagements d'Israël en matière de principes démocratiques".

translation.title: Several European countries are concerned about the Israeli death penalty bill
translation.summary: The Israeli bill to extend the death penalty must be put to the vote in the Knesset on Monday. A text that is of concern to the Foreign Ministers of Germany, France, Italy and the United Kingdom, who believe that this text "would threaten to undermine Israel's commitments to democratic principles".

items.title: En direct : un Casque bleu tué au Liban, Donald Trump dit entrevoir un "accord" avec Téhéran

Il était de nationalité indonésienne. De son côté, Donald Trump a assuré entrevoir un "accord" avec les nouveaux dirigeants iraniens, estimant que la guerre lancée par les États-Unis et Israël avait abouti à un "changement de régime". Suivez notre direct.

items.title: Blocus sur le détroit d'Ormuz : l'accès aux médicaments menacé en Afrique
items.summary: L’Afrique est une victime collatérale de la guerre au Moyen-Orient. Une pénurie de médicaments menace le contient qui importe plus de 70% de ses médicaments, principalement d'Inde et de Chine. En cause, le blocage du détroit d’Ormuz qui rallonge les trajets et augmente le coût du transport.

translation.title: Blocking the Strait of Ormuz: Access to Threatened Medicines in Africa
translation.summary: Africa is a collateral victim of the Middle East war. A shortage of drugs threatens it, which imports more than 70% of its drugs, mainly from India and China. Because of this, the blockade of the Strait of Ormuz which lengthens the journeys and increases the cost of transport.

Twenty-two years ago, with the deployment of Xi Jinping, then Secretary-General of Zhejiang Province, the “millions of projects” focusing on the environmental rehabilitation of rural settlements were launched. At that time, Comrade Xi had been the secretary of the Zhejiang province for more than six months and had been working for a long time. Why does he focus on the rehabilitation of the rural habitat environment? At the grass-roots level, Comrade Xi found that while the Zhejiang economy was growing rapidly, the rural areas were faced with “growth problems” and the villages were less than satisfactory. Out of 34,000 villages in the province, only 4,000 have a better habitat. It was described as “a village after a village, which is full of garbage”. And the farmers described it as: “Getting up early and making money, counting it in the trash, spending it in the hospital.” It can be said that the remediation of the habitat environment was an urgent aspiration of the rural population of Zhejiang at the time. translation.title: Circular on the conduct of the Fourth National Agricultural Census issued by the State Council
translation.summary: Xinhua News Service, Beijing, 5 June In accordance with the Statistical Law of the People ' s Republic of China and the Regulations on the National Agricultural Census, the State Council decided to conduct the Fourth National Agricultural Census in 2026. The Circular stressed that the Fourth National Agricultural Census was a major national survey on the new quest for a comprehensive revival of the Chinese nation through China's modernization, guided by the Chinese socialist ideology of Xi Jinping's new era, and guided by the spirit of the 20th and 20th China-China Congresses, and by the serious implementation of the Party's central and State Council's decision-making process. It was determined that the census should be conducted in accordance with the law, that the scientific census should be conducted and that the census should be conducted for the people's census, that it should be carried out in accordance with the law, that it should be carried out in a realistic and innovative manner, and that the “three farmers” of the new era should be thoroughly examined in order to objectively reflect new developments in agricultural development, new aspects of rural construction, new changes in rural life and new effects of rural reform. The Circular states that the Fourth National Agricultural Survey is aimed at rural households in the People ' s Republic of China, including rural agricultural producers and other households; urban agricultural producers; agricultural production units; villagers ' committees; and town and town people ' s governments. The main elements of the census include agricultural production conditions, food and food production, new quality of agricultural productivity, basics of rural development, living conditions of rural residents, etc. The standard time of the census is 2400 hours on 31 December 2026, and the data for the period is annual 2026. items.title: 中央军委主席习近平签署命令 发布《军事设施建设条例》
items.summary: 新华社北京6月5日电 中央军委主席习近平日前签署命令，发布《军事设施建设条例》，自2025年8月1日起施行。 条例深入贯彻习近平强军思想，深入贯彻新时代军事战略方针，聚焦服务备战打仗，适应新的领导指挥体制和保障模式，科学规范军事设施建设的基本原则、管理体制、运行机制和工作制度，是军事设施建设工作的重要依据。 条例共11章63条，按照军委管总、战区主战、军种主建的总原则，围绕理顺建设链、管理链、监督链，重点从军事设施建设计划管理、项目审批、工程发包、勘察设计、施工管理、应急建设、安全保密、监督问责等方面，厘清管理权限，优化流程机制，固化实践成果，为提高军事设施建设质量效益和保障能力提供有力法治保障。

In accordance with the general principles of the Military Commission ' s General Administration, the main war zone and military construction, the regulations provide for the clarification of management competencies, the optimization of process mechanisms, the consolidation of practical results and the provision of robust rule of law guarantees for the quality and safety of military facilities, in accordance with the general principles of the Military Commission, chapter 11, section 63, and the streamlining of the construction chain, the management chain and the chain of supervision, with emphasis on the management of the military facilities construction plan, project approval, engineering contracting, survey design, construction management, emergency response, security confidentiality and oversight accountability.

(b) To apply the spirit of reform and strict criteria to govern the party, to ensure that the party group (including the party committee of its nature, together with the other party members) is fully accountable to the party ' s principals, to strengthen the monitoring role of the monitoring and oversight bodies (hereinafter referred to as the accredited bodies) and to promote a culture of discipline and anti-corruption with a strict tone, strict measures and strict atmosphere. items.title: 全国人大常委会启动节约能源法执法检查
items.summary: 新华社北京6月5日电 为推动节约能源法全面有效实施，促进更高水平、更高质量做好节能工作，全国人大常委会5日启动节约能源法执法检查。 节约能源法自1998年1月1日实施，并于2007年、2016年、2018年进行了修改。 记者5日从全国人大常委会节约能源法执法检查组第一次全体会议上了解到，为全面深入了解法律实施情况，此次执法检查采取实地检查与委托检查相结合的方式，检查组将赴天津、江苏、湖南、广东、广西、四川等省（区、市）开展实地检查，并委托北京、河北、山西、吉林、上海、重庆等省（市）人大常委会对本行政区域内节约能源法实施情况进行检查。检查组将采取实地检查、听取汇报、组织座谈、典型调研、专题研究、抽查暗访等多种方式，全面准确了解法律实施情况，对带有普遍性的问题深入调查研究。 检查组将重点检查8方面内容：落实节能管理制度情况，推进重点领域节能工作情况，重点用能单位节能管理情况，推动节能技术进步情况，实施节能激励措施情况，与法律配套的行政法规、部门规章以及地方性法规的制定情况，法律实施中存在的其他问题，对节约能源法修改的意见和建议。

translation.title: The Standing Committee of the National People ' s Congress initiated an energy-saving law enforcement inspection
translation.summary: In order to promote the full and effective implementation of the Energy Conservation Act and to promote higher and higher-quality energy efficiency, the Standing Committee of the National People ' s Congress launched an energy conservation law enforcement inspection on 5 June. The Energy Conservation Act has been in force since 1 January 1998 and was amended in 2007, 2016 and 2018. On 5 May, journalists learned from the first plenary meeting of the Standing Committee of the National People ' s Congress ' s Standing Committee on Energy Conservation Law Enforcement Inspection Unit that, in order to obtain a comprehensive and in-depth understanding of the implementation of the law, the inspection took the form of a combination of on-site inspections and commissioned inspections, which will be carried out in the provinces (districts and municipalities) of Tianjin, Jiangsu, Hunan, Guangdong, Guangxi, Sichuan and Beijing, Hebei, Shanxi, Jilin, Shanghai and Chongqing, among others. The Unit will have a comprehensive and accurate understanding of the implementation of the law and will conduct in-depth research and studies on issues of a general nature, in a variety of ways, including on-site inspections, debriefings, organization of talks, typical research, thematic studies, spot-checking and unannounced visits. The Unit will focus on eight areas: implementation of energy-saving management systems; promotion of energy-saving efforts in priority areas; focus on energy-saving management in energy-use units; promotion of technological progress in energy-saving; implementation of energy-saving incentives; development of administrative, sectoral and local regulations in conjunction with the law; other issues in the implementation of the law; and suggestions and recommendations for changes to the energy-saving law.
